notes: SHIP v1.14.245 — CLASSIFIER: UFM is real gear, not BLANK (per HANDOFF-PKG-FINAL-2026-07-13.md Ship 1). John device-confirmed on DFW-05: SPARKS rack s3:176 row `s3-ufm2-r176-us-west-10a` rendered type BLANK. A UFM is NVIDIA Unified Fabric Manager — the appliance managing the InfiniBand fabric (note the four fbs switches directly below it at U11-U20). It must never render as a blanking panel. THREE EDITS. (1) `master_hostType` (:29340) — added `if (/ufm|fabric\s*manager/.test(s)) return 'server';` immediately before the `'other'` fallthrough. Placed at the TAIL so it can never steal a host an earlier, more specific rule already claimed. (2) `_TMAP` (:17779, inside forge3d_render) — added the `server: 'server'` key. (3) `deploy_classifyDevice` (:26630) — same ufm test before its `'blank'` fallthrough. Reuses the existing `server` type end-to-end: ZERO additions to TYPE_COLOR/TLABEL (:17749-17751) or TYPE_COLORS (:39782), ZERO CSS. Renders 'SERVER' with the server dot colour. ⚠️ DEVIATION FROM SPEC — THE SPEC'S ROOT CAUSE NAMED THE WRONG FUNCTION, AND ITS PRESCRIBED FIX WAS A PROVEN NO-OP. The handoff attributes the s3:176 BLANK to `deploy_classifyDevice`. It is not in that path. s3:176 reached the screen through the MASTER (SCOPE A JOB -> mscope_buildRacksFromSnapshot -> master_rackToElevation, which stamps `type: master_hostType(h)`); `deploy_classifyDevice` is only ever fed `cols[3]` by the EDP **CSV** parser (:27728). The real chain is master_hostType -> 'other' -> _TMAP['other'] -> 'blank' -> TLABEL -> BLANK. The spec further told me to verify 'server' against the TYPE_COLORS map — that is the RACK MAP's map (:39782, which does carry server); the map on the FAILING surface is `_TMAP` (:17779), the Forge bridge from master_hostType's vocabulary to the flat one, and it had NO 'server' key. VERIFIED BY EXECUTION: applying the spec's line verbatim and pushing a UFM through the real render chain yields master_hostType->'server', _TMAP['server']->undefined, `|| 'blank'` -> flat type 'blank' -> TLABEL -> **STILL BLANK**. The spec fix ALONE changes nothing on the screen John photographed. Edit (2) is therefore load-bearing, not cosmetic — without it, (1) is inert. `deploy_classifyDevice` is still patched: it is UFM-blind too, same defect class, and a CSV-sourced UFM would hit it. PRE-SHIP GUARD (replayed BOTH real masters through the patched classifiers, extracted verbatim from the built file, run on the app's own vendored SheetJS): TOTAL TYPE DRIFT = **2 hosts, both UFMs** (`s3-ufm1-r175`, `s3-ufm2-r176`; other/blank -> server/server). **DFW02: 0 drift across all 2347 placed hosts.** SPARKS: 0 drift across the other 4141. deploy_classifyDevice token sweep: only ufm / 'fabric manager' strings change; gpu/leaf/mgmt/pdu/patch/console/server/storage/HGX/q3400-ra/''/null all identical. The `_TMAP` addition is inert for existing data (master_hostType could not emit 'server' before this ship). BLANK-TOKEN AUDIT (spec-requested, NO reclassification — JOHN RULES): every distinct model token still typing BLANK on the real masters. **DFW02: 1 of 2347 (0.0%)** — a single row with an EMPTY model cell @c1:001:38. **SPARKS: 461 of 4143 (11.1%), only FOUR tokens** — `q3400-ra` x448 (every one named `s*-ib-ruNN-*`: the master itself calls them InfiniBand — strong, but it is a NAMING INFERENCE, so NOT seeded) · `net-6x100g-02` x6 (these ARE John's `pkey02`/`pkey04`/`metal-jump01` rows — one token explains three of the four BLANKs on his screen) · `fs-media-converter-chassis` x5 · `net-ufm-05` x2 (fixed by this ship). s3:176 reproduced exactly: 4 BLANK rows of 21 (U31 metal-jump01, U29 pkey02, U28 pkey04, U27 ufm2). No speculative reclassification — pkey / metal-jump / q3400-ra / media-converter all still render BLANK pending John's ruling. ⚠️ SYSTEMIC FINDING SURFACED, NOT FIXED (John's call, own ship): `_TMAP.other = 'blank'` means EVERY unclassified Master device renders as a BLANKING PANEL — the app draws 'nothing is installed here' over real hardware. Same honesty class as the 1U-guess that .238 exists to kill, but strictly worse: a wrong height still SHOWS a device; BLANK erases it. That is 461 hosts on SPARKS today. An honest 'unknown' type (gold, like the height flag) rather than 'blank' would close the whole class instead of one token at a time. Also noted in passing, NOT touched (strict scope): in deploy_classifyDevice the `/mgmt/` test precedes the `/cable\s*mgmt/` test, so 'cable mgmt' classifies as 'switch', not 'blank' — pre-existing, unrelated to this ship. Gates: node --check x4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS, CSS brace balance IDENTICAL to .244 (this ship adds no CSS), CRLF preserved, three-stamp lockstep .244 -> .245. ?legacy=1: unaffected — all three edits are pure classifier/lookup logic with no markup, no gating, no CSS; legacy renders the same types it always did, except a UFM now reads SERVER instead of BLANK, which is the entire point and is correct in both houses. BATCH = 1 (.245). Prior batch (.238-.244) CLEARED by John 2026-07-13. DEVICE-VERIFY (John): s3:176 -> UFM row (U27) reads SERVER, not BLANK · pkey02 / pkey04 / metal-jump01 rows UNCHANGED this ship (still BLANK — awaiting your ruling) · one other rack spot-checked for type regressions · ?legacy=1 curl-diff.
